title: RISK: No request-response correlation tracking on JSON-RPC client
description: core/src/transport.rs lines 373-414: parse_incoming() routes responses (messages with id) directly to ClientEvent::Response, matching by resp.id. However, the client application (CLI) has no built-in request tracker. If a response arrives for a stale or cancelled request, or if response order is scrambled, the client has no way to know. Worse: if two sequential GET_KEY_PACKAGE requests are sent with ids 5 and 6, and responses arrive as {id:6} then {id:5}, the CLI code will process them in arrival order and assign the wrong result to the wrong request. No request queue, no id→context map. Risk: out-of-order responses silently assigned to wrong requests; hard to debug.
close_reason: False positive. GetKeyPackageResult carries pub_id in the response, so the client correlates by pub_id, not by request id. This is self-identifying; no correlation map needed.
